original_hypothesis.title: LockingMultiRewards.recover allows owner to drain reward token balances without user protection
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The recover function only enforces skimming limits for the stakingToken. For any other token including registered reward tokens, the owner can transfer the entire balance to themselves with no restriction. This allows the owner to drain reward tokens that are reserved for pending user reward distributions, making the contract insolvent for reward payments.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The code explicitly checks `if (tokenAddress == stakingToken)` for the skimming guard, but has no protection for reward tokens. If tokenAddress is a reward token, the entire balance can be withdrawn regardless of pending reward obligations.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Only stakingToken has balance check; reward tokens can be drained completely via recover(rewardToken, fullBalance)

preconditions: Owner calls recover with a reward token address and large amount
Reward tokens have been deposited via notifyRewardAmount

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: No balance check for non-stakingToken addresses; reward tokens unprotected
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Owner can drain all reward token balances, making pending rewards unclaimable
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: Missing: check that recovered amount doesn't exceed surplus (balance - owed rewards) for reward tokens

analysis_questions: What is the total reward token balance at any given time?
How much is owed to users in pending/locked rewards?
Is there a governance timelock protecting the owner key?
validation_requirements: Verify users can no longer claim rewards after drain
Test that notifyRewardAmount still works but rewards are insolvent

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: A compromised or malicious owner calls recover(rewardTokenAddress, rewardToken.balanceOf(address(this))). All reward tokens are transferred to the owner. Users who call getRewards() after this will either get reverts (insufficient balance) or receive nothing. The rewardRate still indicates pending rewards but the contract has no tokens to pay them.
prerequisites: Owner key compromised or malicious owner action

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Complete loss of all accumulated and pending reward tokens for all users
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All stakers with pending or locked rewards
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Owner could re-deposit reward tokens; otherwise permanent loss

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test for recover() with reward token addresses

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Detectable via token transfer events from contract to owner

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
